text:
Once upon a time there was a little red owl named Olive who loved listening to old owlish legends.

One dusk she perched by a fireplace. "Did you hear about the wise old night hawk?" she crooned. "He could see miles into forests."

Olive chirped thoughtfully. "And brave?"

"Awe brav."

"A brave wise old hawk?"

"A year ago he soared over a field. A rabbit darted out. He didn’t stop."

Olive felt sorrowful. "I wish…"

He didn't care.







"Aren’t you just?"

"Of course."

"The sun dipped low. The hawk flew home."

"She rests?"

"She dreams."

Next morning she heard her. "You could've flown!"

"Hawks don’t dream."

"Oh."





Olive smiled. "*You're* beautiful.

A year.



*You*

*still love* those big fields?

"*You* do.

You're still out there."

"Oh. Yes, alone."








*You
